title: malva thuringiaca subsp. thuringiaca
intro: Malva thuringiaca subsp. thuringiaca, also known as Marsh Mallow or Root of the Sea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which are attributed to its rich content of mucilages, a type of complex carbohydrate. The health benefits of Malva thuringiaca subsp. thuringiaca include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation and irritation in the skin, and even provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, Malva thuringiaca subsp. thuringiaca has been prepared as a herbal infusion, decoction, or syrup, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
